changes_made: Renamed k8s_rbac_oidc_authentication_configured to k8s_apiserver_oidc_mfa_enforced for clarity and accuracy
review_notes: The control of leveraging multi-factor authentication is applicable to Kubernetes through the use of an external identity provider like OIDC that supports MFA. The original function names were slightly misleading and did not fully align with the specific implementation details in Kubernetes. The revised function name better reflects the use of OIDC for MFA enforcement at the API server level.
naming_improvements: Ensure function names clearly indicate the specific mechanism or configuration being enforced, such as OIDC for MFA.
title: Leverage multi-factor authentication for all users
step1_reasoning: The control 'Leverage multi-factor authentication for all users' is about ensuring strong authentication mechanisms. In Kubernetes, while there is no direct MFA implementation, you can configure the API server to use an external identity provider (OIDC) that supports MFA. Additionally, enforcing MFA can be part of the authentication mechanisms configured for accessing the Kubernetes API server.
